V11X2-CG-01.risk: May overlap with existing overhead recline or floor lounge poses. Keep only if the top-down spiral S-curve is clearly distinct.
V11X2-CG-01.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-02.risk: High risk. This may overlap with the existing kneeling floor-hand goddess pose / Centered Kneeling Floor-Hand Goddess Pose. Keep only if high-angle framing, forward lean, and hand placement are distinct enough. Otherwise skip or merge and report the reason.
V11X2-CG-02.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-03.risk: May overlap with low-angle reaching hand glamour poses. Keep if the floor-level kneeling-above-viewer staging and foreground hand perspective are distinct.
V11X2-CG-03.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-04.risk: May overlap with low-angle runway or low-angle power-curve poses. Keep if it preserves true worm's-eye vertical standing arch.
V11X2-CG-04.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-05.risk: May overlap with rear low-angle over-shoulder poses. Keep if this version is less extreme and clearly rear three-quarter editorial.
V11X2-CG-05.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-06.risk: May overlap with existing side-recline or floor-lounge poses. Keep if the low side camera and full S-curve editorial bodyline are distinct.
V11X2-CG-06.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-07.risk: May overlap with general seated twist poses. Keep if platform-edge staging is part of the pose identity.
V11X2-CG-07.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-08.risk: May overlap with floating divine poses. Keep if camera tilt and diagonal fashion framing are the primary feature, not combat flight.
V11X2-CG-08.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-09.risk: May overlap with close lens reaching hand poses. Keep if it frames the face rather than reaching toward the viewer.
V11X2-CG-09.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-10.risk: May overlap with low-angle runway/power poses. Keep if raised platform or stair step-up staging is clear.
V11X2-CG-10.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-11.risk: May overlap with overhead recline poses, but keep if compact crescent C-curve is distinct from long diagonal S-curve.
V11X2-CG-11.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-12.risk: May overlap with side-lying editorial curve. Keep only if hip-level low side camera and lifted hip-pop bodyline remain distinct.
V11X2-CG-12.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-13.risk: May overlap with overhead S-curve or high-angle recline poses. Keep if the upward back arch is the unique staging.
V11X2-CG-13.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-14.risk: May overlap with low-angle seated lounge poses. Keep if worm's-eye floor camera and dominant seated sprawl are distinct.
V11X2-CG-14.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-15.risk: High overlap risk with CG-05. Keep only if this version is more extreme and clearly behind-below overlook. Otherwise skip or merge with CG-05.
V11X2-CG-15.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-16.risk: This is close-up character-selling / portrait glamour more than body showcase. Keep only if the library needs a strong hand-framed expression pose. In reports, classify it as close-up character-selling within camera-driven glamour, not as body showcase.
V11X2-CG-16.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-17.risk: May overlap with waist-up glamour twist poses. Keep if tight crop and opposing chest/hip angle are distinct.
V11X2-CG-17.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-18.risk: May overlap with CG-01 and CG-11. Keep only if extended leg as diagonal frame axis is clearly the unique feature.
V11X2-CG-18.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-19.risk: May overlap with side-lying and low-side hip-pop poses. Keep if side high-angle folded-leg structure is distinct.
V11X2-CG-19.decision: kept - similar but distinct camera/framing/body staging

V11X2-CG-20.risk: May overlap with low-angle runway, low-angle reaching, or sitting sprawl poses. Keep if extreme foreshortening and foreground knee/leg staging are distinct.
